_description: Synthetic destatusing test scenarios for Rules A through F. All dates are synthetic. Calendar: Mon-Fri, 8h/day. new_dd = 2024-01-10 (Wednesday), old_dd = 2024-01-17 (Wednesday). Workday table: 2024-01-01 through 2024-01-31.

rule_a._note: CALC-005: AS and AF both before new_dd. Do nothing.

rule_b._note: CALC-006: AS and AF both after new_dd, before old_dd. Remove AS/AF, OD=AD, PC=0.

rule_c._note: CALC-007: ES and EF both after old_dd. Do nothing.

rule_d._note: CALC-008: AS before new_dd, AF after new_dd. Remove AF. RD = workdays(new_dd, old_AF). WF-06: PC = AD_before/(AD_before+RD) where AD_before = wd(AS->new_dd). new_dd=2024-01-10(wd=8), old_AF=2024-01-17(wd=13). RD=13-8=5. AD_before=wd(1/8->1/10)=2. PC=2/7=0.2857 (caller AD=3 no longer used for PC).

rule_e._note: CALC-009: AS between new_dd and old_dd, EF after old_dd. Remove AS. OD=AD+RD. PC=0.

rule_f._note: CALC-010: AS before new_dd, EF after old_dd. Compute RD and PC from new_dd to old EF. new_dd=2024-01-10(wd=8), old_EF=2024-01-24(wd=18). RD=18-8=10. AD=5. PC=5/15=0.3333...
